---
title: "2.9 Implementing Selection and Iteration Algorithms"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### Syntax becomes reusable algorithm patterns

This topic combines selection and iteration to implement common AP CSA algorithms.

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">accumulator</span> pattern for sum and average
- <span class="term">sentinel</span> loops for input
- minimum and maximum search
- divisibility and digit extraction
- random choice and frequency counters
:::

## Accumulator Pattern

### Sum and average share the same structure

```java
int sum = 0;
for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++)
{
    sum += i;
}
```

Pattern:

::: {.tight-list}
- initialize the accumulator before the loop
- update it inside the loop
- use it after the loop
:::

Average adds a final division step.

## Code Task

### Complete sum and average

```java
public static int sum(int n)
{
    int sum = 0;
    for (int i = 1; i <= n; i++)
    {
        sum += i;
    }
    return sum;
}

public static double average(int n)
{
    int total = sum(n);
    return (double) total / n;
}
```

For `n = 10`, expected results are `55` and `5.5`.

## Input-Controlled Loop

### Sentinel input combines sum and count

```java
Scanner scan = new Scanner(System.in);
int sum = 0;
int count = 0;
int number = scan.nextInt();

while (number != -1)
{
    sum += number;
    count++;
    number = scan.nextInt();
}
double average = (double) sum / count;
```

The sentinel `-1` is not included in the average.

## Min and Max

### Selection inside a loop tracks best-so-far

```java
int max = Integer.MIN_VALUE;
for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
{
    int num = (int)(Math.random() * 100);
    if (num > max)
    {
        max = num;
    }
}
```

The variable `max` stores the best value found so far.

## Divisibility

### Prime checking uses `%`

::: {.code-scroll .compact}
```java
public static boolean isPrime(int n)
{
    if (n <= 1)
    {
        return false;
    }

    for (int divisor = 2; divisor < n; divisor++)
    {
        if (n % divisor == 0)
        {
            return false;
        }
    }
    return true;
}
```
:::

The loop returns `false` as soon as it finds a divisor.

## Digits with `/` and `%`

### Peel off the last digit repeatedly

```java
public static boolean checkDigit(int number, int digit)
{
    while (number > 0)
    {
        int lastDigit = number % 10;
        if (lastDigit == digit)
        {
            return true;
        }
        number = number / 10;
    }
    return false;
}
```

`% 10` gets the last digit; `/ 10` removes it.

## Math.random with Selection

### Probabilities come from ranges

The weather report says a 25% chance of rain.

```java
if (Math.random() < 0.25)
{
    System.out.println("Rain");
}
else
{
    System.out.println("No Rain");
}
```

Equivalent condition for rain:

```java
Math.random() > 0.75
```

because values above `0.75` are the top 25% of the range.

## Frequency

### Count how often a condition is true

```java
int count = 0;
for (int i = 1; i <= 100; i++)
{
    if (i % 2 == 0)
    {
        count++;
    }
}
```

Frequency algorithms use a counter inside a loop and increment only when the condition is true.

## Groupwork Coding Challenge {.fit-small}

### Prime Number Finder

Complete `findPrimes` by reusing `isPrime`.

```java
public static int findPrimes(int num1, int num2)
{
    int count = 0;
    for (int n = num1; n <= num2; n++)
    {
        if (isPrime(n))
        {
            System.out.println(n);
            count++;
        }
    }
    return count;
}
```

For `findPrimes(2, 100)`, the expected count is `25`.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- implement sum and average using an accumulator
- use a sentinel loop without including the sentinel in the data
- update min or max only when a better value is found
- use `%` and `/` for divisibility and digit extraction
- combine selection and loops for frequency and prime-number algorithms
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
